subject: Mathematics
title: The Arrow Paradox
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Arrow Paradox.

hook.text: At any single instant, a flying arrow occupies exactly one position. It isn't moving.

buildup.text: If the arrow is still at every instant, and time is made of instants, when does motion happen?

discovery.text: Zeno concluded that motion is an illusion. Reality is a sequence of frozen frames.

twist.text: Modern physics describes velocity at an instant using derivatives—motion without duration.

climax.text: The arrow paradox pushed thinkers toward the concept of instantaneous rate of change.

punchline.text: Calculus explains how something can be moving at a single frozen instant.

quiz.question: What concept resolves the arrow paradox?
